description_long: This is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular, slightly curved wooden body and a glossy, dark brown finish. The front panel features a cream-colored faceplate with two large round dials on either side, a horizontal frequency indicator window, and several push buttons below. The speaker grille is covered with a woven mesh fabric, positioned above the controls and frequency display. The back panel is metallic with ventilation holes, technical labeling, and connection ports, including a visible model number and power information. The sides and top are smooth, uninterrupted wood, giving the object a classic, mid-20th-century appearance. This radio would typically be found in homes, shops, or urban gathering spaces, serving as both a functional appliance and a decorative piece.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a cream front panel, two large dials, a mesh speaker grille, and a metallic back with ventilation holes and labeling.
description_view_0: The left side view shows a smooth, curved wooden surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_1: The front view displays the cream faceplate, two large dials, a mesh speaker grille, a frequency indicator, and several push buttons.
description_view_2: The right side view mirrors the left, showing a continuous, polished wooden surface without any controls.
description_view_3: The back view reveals a metallic panel with ventilation holes, technical labeling, connection ports, and the model number.
